query: I need to run three claude agents on three different feature branches without re-cloning the repo each time.
spin up a fresh checkout of pr-123 alongside my current main so I can build it without switching.
open the feature-auth branch in a separate working directory, I don't want to stash my changes.

remove that stale worktree I made yesterday for the hotfix branch.
run npm test in a separate checkout without touching what I'm working on right now.
clean up any cached reference trees on disk that nothing is using anymore.
show me all the linked working trees for this repo.
I want to try this patch in an isolated copy of the repo; keep my dirty tree alone.
give me another checkout of main so I can run the build there while I edit here.
create a new branch called feature-auth off main.
I need to clone a fresh copy of the HikaruEgashira/gh-wt repo from GitHub.
list all branches in this repo, including remote ones.
what's the conceptual difference between `git worktree` and `git clone`?
delete the feature-auth branch — both local and remote.
how much disk space is my .git directory using?
switch my current checkout to the feature-auth branch.
run `git gc --aggressive` to clean up unreachable objects.
open main.py in VS Code.
install the github cli on this mac.
